findings.description: Thomas Richison (also recorded Richardson / Richeson) was the father of John Richardson. Born 18 February 1804 in Wigan, Lancashire, England; died 3 November 1886 in Richmond, Cache County, Utah. Father and son share a documented Nauvoo residence (1839–1846) and a joint 1852 emigration to Utah, which bridges John's English origin to his Utah life.
supporting_sources: Nauvoo Community Project (BYU Center for Family History and Genealogy) — the Richardson/Richison family in Nauvoo, Hancock County, Illinois, 1839–1846.
LDS Church membership and Utah Mormon Pioneer Overland Travel records placing Thomas and John Richardson in the same emigrating household.

findings.description: Mary Mallalieu (married name Richardson) was the mother of John Richardson. Born 16 September 1806 in Chorley, Lancashire, England; died 9 March 1846 in Nauvoo, Hancock County, Illinois — before the family reached Utah, so she is absent from every later Utah record. Recovering her requires early Nauvoo community records and English (Chorley) parish registers.
supporting_sources: Nauvoo Community Project (BYU Center for Family History and Genealogy) — Mary (Mallalieu) Richardson's residence and 1846 death at Nauvoo, Hancock County, Illinois.
England, Births and Christenings — christening of Mary Mallalieu, Chorley, Lancashire, 1806.
